channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Gig Companies are backing Trump’s Labor Secretary nominee. Here’s what that means for workers
item.description: The trade association representing America’s largest gig companies is backing President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Labor—an endorsement that could shape the future of worker classification in the gig economy.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Siemens announces $285 investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scam alert: This is how thieves might try to steal your tax refund or other money
item.description: The IRS just released its "dirty dozen," an annual list of the top 12 ways tax filers could get ripped off. It also lists common mistakes to avoid making when filing taxes.
item.dc:creator: Roxana Popescu, The Mercury News

item.title: How to catch a Gravid Bowfin in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find a Gravid Bowfin in Monster Hunter Wilds, including some info on catching it once you do.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IT industry workers hit badly by burnout, stress - but there's still potential for success
item.description: Most IT workers have experienced burnout, with heavy workloads, tight deadlines and a lack of resources all contributing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Utah just passed the country’s first age-verification bill for app stores. But the fight isn’t over
item.description: On Wednesday, Utah became the first state in the country to pass legislation mandating that app stores verify users’ ages and get parental consent for certain activity on minors’ accounts.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Ecco come i leader IT si preparano alla grande sfida per la gestione degli agenti AI
item.description: Via via che gli AI agent arriveranno a occupare porzioni sempre più significative dei flussi di lavoro aziendali, i leader IT dovranno affrontare una sfida sempre più complessa: gestirli.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tariffs will doom Trump’s attempt to pump crypto
item.description: For cryptocurrency investors, Donald Trump represents a great hope. In the libertarian paradise where tokens on blockchains pave the future of the financial system instead of banks, lenders, and brokerages, Trump could even be a prophet.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Federal judge blocks drastic funding cuts to medical research
item.description: A federal judge is preventing the Trump administration from drastically cutting medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pentagon signs AI deal to aid military decision-making
item.description: The Department of Defense has signed a contract with start-up Scale AI to use artificial intelligence for military planning and operations, marking the Pentagon's latest incorporation of emerging tech into its workflows.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How CIOs can navigate a perfect digital storm of complexity, competition, and regulation
item.description: The banking, financial services, and insurance (BFSI) sector is facing a storm. In recent decades, the widespread adoption of the internet and the subsequent smartphone revolution have empowered consumers and businesses to look beyond incumbent providers of financial services.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk to speak to Senate Republicans on Wednesday
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk, the moving force behind the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), will meet with Senate Republicans on Wednesday to answer their questions and concerns about his aggressive efforts to shrink the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Embrace the chaos: A messy Windows productivity system is actually perfect
item.description: I have a confession: My productivity system is a bit of a mess. When I see people sharing beautifully organized Notion dashboards and using the latest subscription-based productivity tools, I often wince.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to boost Python program performance with Zig
item.description: Python might not be the fastest of languages, but it has many advantages. Users enjoy Python’s convenience and development speed, as well as its broad ecosystem of libraries. Something else people appreciate about Python is how it bridges language ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How eBPF is changing container networking
item.description: “No man is an island,” wrote John Donne. But containers certainly are. They are created as islands in and of themselves, packaged with all they need to operate independently—code, dependencies, and runtime. This autonomy is great for security but not so great for networking.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve, to boost the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Extra cash with no catch? Spotting retiree job scams
item.description: Reports of task scams have significantly risen in the past four years, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some red flags.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soft robotic prosthetic hand uses nerve signals for more natural control
item.description: Researchers unveiled a new soft prosthetic hand that combines the natural coordination patterns of our fingers with the decoding of motoneuron activity in the spinal column.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How to restore a deleted text message on iPhone
item.description: Accidentally deleting a text message on your iPhone or iPad is not a problem if you still need the message. Apple has a built-in way to recover it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
